growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: ADBE is down 43% from its 52-week high and only 16% off its low while still producing 28.7% net margins; the September 10, 2026 estimated Q3 earnings catalyst will test AI ARR monetization and leadership-transition anxiety after CFO Dan Durn's exit and Narayen's planned succession.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-margin, cash-rich software franchise is priced like AI disruption is already structural, but Q2 results, raised FY26 guidance, AI-first ARR growth, and insider support argue the selloff is overdone.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner

growth_picks.analyst_take: ServiceNow is an overlap BUY after a nearly 50% drawdown because Q1 subscription/cRPO growth remains above 20%, AI monetization is scaling, and Q2 earnings on 2026-07-22 can reset sentiment if execution holds.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Finviz shows INTU still down 66% from its 52-week high and only 9% above its low, yet it remains profitable with 21.9% net margin; the August 20, 2026 estimated Q4/FY2026 earnings catalyst should test whether the 17% workforce cut and raised FY2026 EPS guidance are real margin repair or evidence of AI-led demand erosion.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner

growth_picks.analyst_take: AI-driven DRAM/HBM shortage, record Q3 results, and multi-year strategic customer agreements support further upside, but the post-rally entry risk argues against chasing it as a STRONG BUY.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: NATO's push into drones, command-and-control, and air and missile defense, alongside Space Force missile-warning budget momentum for Golden Dome, directly supports L3Harris missile-tracking satellite and defense communications franchises.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Defense-cycle and missile-warning exposure are supported by record Q1 backlog, but valuation and cash-flow timing make this a pullback buy rather than a chase.

catalysts_general: Discovery: NVIDIA's Q1 2026 13F showed a new strategic Coherent position of 7.79M shares worth about $1.86B, tying an AI infrastructure leader to photonics supply-chain exposure.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Coherent has credible AI optical-networking upside from NVIDIA-backed capacity expansion and accelerating datacenter demand, but valuation and cash-flow risk argue for disciplined sizing.

catalysts_general: Discovery: CEO Perry A. Sook bought 12,235 shares on June 26, 2026 at $162.2647, committing about $1.99M of personal capital and taking direct ownership to 899,044 shares.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap forward earnings, strong free cash flow, election-year political ad upside, and a fresh $2M CEO open-market buy support upside, but TEGNA leverage and litigation keep conviction below high.

catalysts_general: Discovery: BAH is down roughly 48% from its 52-week high and only 5% above its low, with the stock recently printing a 52-week low despite defense/AI positioning; the July 24, 2026 Q1 FY2027 earnings call is a near-term catalyst for whether Civil softness is temporary and National Security/defense AI margins can carry the reset.
growth_picks.analyst_take: BAH is a cheap, cash-generative defense/AI contractor near 52-week lows, with July earnings and fresh OpenAI/defense-tech actions as catalysts, but Civil weakness and government-contract risk cap conviction.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Space Force's missile-warning and tracking architecture needs ground operations and data transport, and Kratos' reported $447 million Space Force missile-tracking ground-system follow-on makes it a direct small-cap beneficiary of Golden Dome execution.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Kratos is a direct defense-space and unmanned-systems growth beneficiary after a major Space Force missile-tracking ground-system award and Q1 beat-and-raise, but valuation and cash burn cap conviction.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Russian uranium restrictions and the strategic need for non-Russian HALEU supply are turning domestic enrichment capacity into a national-security bottleneck, with Centrus positioned as a direct supplier to advanced nuclear customers.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Fresh DOE HALEU contract and domestic enrichment scarcity create a credible multi-year catalyst, but valuation, capex, and cash-flow risk keep this below strong-buy quality.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Persian Gulf tanker rates nearly doubled as producers rushed to clear stranded crude after Hormuz disruptions, creating a spot-rate earnings catalyst for product and crude tanker operators that may persist if Red Sea and Gulf security risks remain unstable.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap product-tanker operator with strong Q1 earnings, high near-term TCE rates, buyback/dividend support, and a July earnings catalyst, but cyclicality and recent insider sales keep conviction below high.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Director Carlos Alberini bought 11,388 RH shares on June 29, 2026 across three open-market purchase lines, spending about $1.83M through his trust.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Contrarian luxury-home turnaround with a fresh $1.83M director buy, raised FY2026 guidance, high FCF yield, and heavy short interest, but only attractive on a pullback near the insider purchase zone.

catalysts_general: Discovery: CLO and Executive Officer Julio Cesar Bueno bought 110,441 preference shares on July 1, 2026 at roughly $18.10, a near-$2.0M insider commitment in a large-cap bank ADR.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap Brazilian bank turnaround with nine quarters of recurring profit growth and a fresh $2M-equivalent executive open-market purchase, but buy discipline matters because upside is only moderate at the current ADR price.

catalysts_general: Discovery: China's entity-specific dual-use export controls on USA Rare Earth make the company's U.S. mine-to-magnet buildout more strategically valuable, though execution and litigation risk keep the opportunity speculative.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Speculative U.S. rare-earth supply-chain buildout with major government-backed capital and near-term operational milestones, but only suitable below current levels given dilution, litigation, and execution risk.
High-margin FICO franchise is producing exceptional FY2026 growth and cash flow after a 36% drawdown, but mortgage credit-score competition caps conviction.

AppLovin's extreme profitability and newly opened self-serve ad platform create a near-term catalyst, but valuation, volatility, insider selling, and regulatory scrutiny cap conviction.
Cheap gold producer with strong margins, net cash and major Island Gold growth, but recent Young-Davidson issues make the July guidance reset a key risk.
High-quality medtech franchise reset to value multiples after a severe drawdown, with Q2 earnings and cardiovascular pipeline/M&A updates as near-term catalysts.

High-margin animal-health leader is priced like a broken franchise after a 54% drawdown, but positive FCF, cheap forward earnings, insider buying, and an August Q2 catalyst make the risk/reward attractive if guidance stabilizes.
Deep value cable turnaround with strong FCF, heavy short interest, insider buying, and near-term Cox/SpaceX/Q2 catalysts, but broadband losses and leverage keep this below strong-buy quality.
Cheap scanner-only payments name with Q1 beat/raised guidance, activist-driven governance refresh, and a $1B buyback, offset by high leverage and modest growth.
Beaten-down spine-device grower with improving EBITDA/FCF profile, a fresh debt refinance, insider buying, and a near-term Q2 earnings catalyst, but leverage and GAAP losses cap conviction.

Beaten-down Workday offers a rare mix of software-quality margins, double-digit subscription growth, and high FCF yield at a depressed forward multiple, but AI disruption and leadership-transition risk cap conviction.
Alphabet is not a deep-value setup, but strong Q1 execution, accelerating AI/cloud demand, high margins, large FCF, and a near-term Q2 earnings catalyst support buying only on disciplined pullbacks.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner

catalysts_general: Discovery: Brookfield expanded its AI infrastructure partnership with Bloom to $25 billion, making Bloom a direct onsite-power beneficiary as AI factories look for fast grid-independent capacity.

Strong Q1 2026 distributable cash flow and a covered 6%+ distribution are offset by high leverage, thin margins, limited analyst upside, and no clear value discount.
Q1 execution, B2B growth, FCF, and buybacks are strong, but the stock is only modestly below its 52-week high with limited target-price upside and macro/CFO-transition risk.

catalysts_general: Discovery: WPP is an avoid candidate because AI-enabled creative production and media optimization are pressuring traditional agency economics while the company restructures to become lower-cost and AI-enabled.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap forward earnings are outweighed by broad organic revenue decline, client-loss overhang, weak cash flow, and unresolved AI-driven agency-model pressure.

catalysts_general: Discovery: UBS upgraded the stock while roughly 27% of the float was short and days-to-cover stood near 5.8, creating squeeze mechanics after better-than-feared earnings and analyst target hikes.

High-quality franchise with strong recent earnings and buybacks, but at ~32x forward earnings, only ~2% target upside, and just 2.8% below its 52-week high, the risk/reward is unattractive for new money.
